name: Dick Cavett
abstract: Richard Alva Cavett ( KAV-it; born November 19, 1936) is an American television personality, comedian and former talk show host. He appeared regularly on nationally broadcast television in the United States from the 1960s through the 2000s.

concerts.label: The New York Pops Second Birthday Gala Celebration
A Cabaret Concert for Jan Wallman
Judy Garland Stamp First Day of Issue Ceremony

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: Cavett came along and his manager... said, 'Dick is going to have a television show at ABC, would you be interested in being the leader?' And I thought I was going to kiss him on the lips, I said, 'Yes!'
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: So I just got called for the Dick Cavett Show. So when he came to do “The Dick Cavett Show” with Mercer and Cootie, he was on the show, and I knew he was coming... I said “hey, would you sign them things for me?” He said, “would you speak out, I can use the publicity.” So he signed the sheets.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Karl Berger
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] When we first came to the U.S. for the first time I saw Jimi Hendrix on television. Was it “The Dick Cavett Show?” >> [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, “The Dick Cavett Show.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Karl Berger interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 2
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Cavett was looking at this, and he got just almost to that point, and he said, “I know where this is going, who did this?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 2 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You were doing Dick Cavett’s television show. ... [INTERVIEWEE] That’s right. I was scared to death.
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Let me first use a line that Dick Cavett said... Dick Cavett once had Lester Maddox on his show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]
